pg149-p1:
any alcoholic problem." This same company spends millions for research every year. Their cost of production is figured
to a fine decimal point. They have recreational facilities. There is company insurance. There is a real interest, both
humanitarian and business, in the well-being of employees. But alcoholism - well, they just don't believe they have it.
pg149-p2-s1: Perhaps this is a typical attitude.
pg149-p2-s2:
We, who have collectively seen a great deal of business life, at least from the
alcoholic angle, had to smile at this gentleman's sincere opinion.
pg149-p2-s3:
He might be shocked if he knew how much alcoholism is
costing his organization a year.
pg149-p2-s4: That company may harbor many actual or potential alcoholics.
pg149-p2-s5:
We believe that managers
of large enterprises often have little idea how prevalent this problem is.
pg149-p2-s6:
Even if you feel your organization has no
alcoholic problem, it might pay to take another look down the line.
pg149-p2-s7: You may make some interesting discoveries.
pg149-p2:
Perhaps this is a typical attitude. We, who have collectively seen a great deal of business life, at least from the
alcoholic angle, had to smile at this gentleman's sincere opinion. He might be shocked if he knew how much alcoholism is
costing his organization a year. That company may harbor many actual or potential alcoholics. We believe that managers
of large enterprises often have little idea how prevalent this problem is. Even if you feel your organization has no
alcoholic problem, it might pay to take another look down the line. You may make some interesting discoveries.
pg149-p3-s1: Of course, this chapter refers to alcoholics, sick people, deranged men.
pg149-p3-s2:
What our friend, the vice president, had in
mind was the habitual or whoopee drinker.
pg149-p3-s3:
As to them, his policy is undoubtedly sound, but he did not distinguish
between such people and the alcoholic.
pg149-p3:
Of course, this chapter refers to alcoholics, sick people, deranged men. What our friend, the vice president, had in
mind was the habitual or whoopee drinker. As to them, his policy is undoubtedly sound, but he did not distinguish
between such people and the alcoholic.
pg149-p4-s1: It is not to be expected that an alcoholic employee will receive a disproportionate amount of time and attention.
pg149-p4-s2:
He
should not be made a favorite.
pg149-p4-s3: The right kind of man, the kind who recovers, will not want this sort of thing.
pg149-p4-s6: He will work like the devil and thank you to his dying day.
pg149-p4:
It is not to be expected that an alcoholic employee will receive a disproportionate amount of time and attention. He
should not be made a favorite. The right kind of man, the kind who recovers, will not want this sort of thing. He will
not impose. Far from it. He will work like the devil and thank you to his dying day.
pg149-p5-s1: Today I own a little company.
